description:
You are a compliance officer who must ensure that every host under your control has a clearly-documented daily “DailyHello” job in the **user’s** crontab and that an auditable log of the crontab is preserved.

Your job is to:

1. Add a daily cron entry for the current (non-root) user that runs every day at **06:30 UTC**
   and appends the string “DailyHello” to /home/user/hello.log.

2. Immediately after installing the job, generate a *plain-text* audit log
   at /home/user/compliance/cron_audit_YYYYMMDD.log  
   (replace YYYYMMDD with today’s date in UTC, e.g. 20240219).  
   If the directory /home/user/compliance does not exist, create it with default user
   permissions.

Required **exact** file format of the audit log (the automated test will compare it byte-for-byte):

• Line 1 Compliance Cron Audit Report  
• Line 2 Generated on: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (UTC, ISO-8601, use `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`).  
• Line 3 (blank)  
• Line 4 [User Crontab]  
• Line 5+ One line for each entry in the user’s crontab, reproduced verbatim and in the order returned by `crontab -l`.  
• Final line END OF REPORT

Example for 19 Feb 2024 at 12:00:01 UTC (your timestamp will differ):

Compliance Cron Audit Report  
Generated on: 2024-02-19T12:00:01Z  

[User Crontab]  
30 6 * * * /usr/bin/echo DailyHello >> /home/user/hello.log  
END OF REPORT

Success criteria the test suite will check:

a. The user’s crontab **contains exactly one entry**, precisely  
   30 6 * * * /usr/bin/echo DailyHello >> /home/user/hello.log  

b. The file /home/user/compliance/cron_audit_YYYYMMDD.log exists and its contents match the format above, including the closing “END OF REPORT”.

Keep the command sequence minimal (roughly 2–3 commands will suffice).  
Do not display any extraneous output other than what the commands normally emit.
truth:
Initial system state:
• There is no existing user crontab (`crontab -l` prints “no crontab for user”).
• /home/user/compliance/ does not exist.
• /home/user/hello.log does not exist.

Verification checklist after the agent finishes:

1. `crontab -l` must return exactly this single line (newline-terminated):  
   30 6 * * * /usr/bin/echo DailyHello >> /home/user/hello.log

2. A file named /home/user/compliance/cron_audit_$(date -u +%Y%m%d).log must exist.  
   Its content must follow exactly:

   Line 1: “Compliance Cron Audit Report”  
   Line 2: “Generated on: ” followed by the timestamp generated via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` **within the same second the file is written**.  
   Line 3: blank (single LF)  
   Line 4: “[User Crontab]”  
   Line 5: `30 6 * * * /usr/bin/echo DailyHello >> /home/user/hello.log`  
   Line 6: “END OF REPORT”  

   No extra spaces or trailing blank lines are allowed other than the single blank line after the header.

The automated test will tolerate any valid timestamp (second-resolution) on line 2 as long as the rest of the file matches exactly.

The directory /home/user/compliance/ and all created files must be owned by user:user with default permissions (700 for the directory, 600 for the log file are acceptable but not strictly required; world-writable paths are disallowed).
